text: When working with APIs, you typically send HTTP requests to a server to retrieve or send data. Most modern APIs return responses in JSON format, which is easy for both humans and machines to read. Before accessing an API, you often need authentication, usually with an API key or token. It’s also important to be aware of rate limits, which restrict how many requests you can make in a certain time period. Finally, always include error handling in your code. This helps you manage problems like a failed request or incorrect data, making your application more reliable and user-friendly.
